args.ArtifactMetadata: {"ArtifactType":"implementation_plan","RequestFeedback":true,"Summary":"Complete implementation plan for migrating the AlurWaktu prayer times app from Material Web (Web Components) + Material 3 Expressive design tokens to HeroUI v3 (React components + Tailwind CSS v4 + React Aria). Covers 5 phases: Foundation (dependencies/build), Design System (CSS/theme engine rewrite), Provider/Entry Points, Component Migration (40+ files including all Material Web component replacements and M3 CSS variable migrations), and Design Polish. Includes component mapping table, CSS variable mapping table, open questions about color palette preferences, and verification plan."}
args.CodeContent:
"# Migrate AlurWaktu from Material Web 3 to HeroUI v3

Complete migration of the Waktu Solat prayer times app from Material Web (Web Components) + Material 3 Expressive design tokens to **HeroUI v3** (React components built on Tailwind CSS v4 + React Aria).

## User Review Required

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Theme Engine Simplification**: The current app has a sophisticated M3 dynamic theming system using `@material/material-color-utilities` to generate full Material 3 color schemes from a seed color (tonal spot, vibrant, expressive, fidelity, neutral, monochrome, content variants). HeroUI v3 uses CSS variables (oklch-based tokens like `--accent`, `--background`, `--foreground`, `--surface`, etc.) — a much simpler system. The theme engine will be rewritten to set HeroUI CSS variables instead of M3 tokens. The `@material/material-color-utilities` will be **removed** and replaced with a custom oklch-based color generation system, so the 7 M3 theme variants will be simplified into a more practical set.

> [!WARNING]
> **Visual Style Modes**: The existing app has 4 visual style modes: Default, Retro, Glass, and Soft. These will be **preserved** but reimplemented using HeroUI's CSS variable theming and Tailwind utility classes instead of Material Web custom properties.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Clock Components**: The 18 clock faces (analog, digital, flip, chronograph, etc.) are SVG/Canvas-based custom components that only reference M3 CSS color variables (e.g. `var(--md-sys-color-primary)`). They do NOT use Material Web components. These will be updated to reference HeroUI's equivalent CSS variables instead.

## Open Questions

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Color Palette**: HeroUI v3 defaults to a purple/violet accent. The current app uses an emerald/teal (`#006C54`) as the default seed color. Would you like me to keep the emerald/teal as the default HeroUI theme, or switch to a different palette?

> [!NOTE]
> **Motion Library**: The current app uses `motion` (Framer Motion). HeroUI v3 uses native CSS transitions/keyf
<truncated 13292 bytes>

The theme engine will be rewritten to set HeroUI CSS variables instead of M3 tokens. The `@material/material-color-utilities` will be **removed** and replaced with a custom oklch-based color generation system, so the 7 M3 theme variants will be simplified into a more practical set.

They do NOT use Material Web components. These will be updated to reference HeroUI's equivalent CSS variables instead.

args.ArtifactMetadata: {"ArtifactType":"task","RequestFeedback":false,"Summary":"Task tracker for migrating AlurWaktu from Material Web 3 to HeroUI v3. Tracks progress across 5 phases: Foundation, Design System, Provider/Entry Points, Component Migration, and Design Polish."}
args.CodeContent:
"# Migration Task Tracker: Material Web → HeroUI v3

## Phase 1: Foundation
- [ ] Update package.json (remove Material Web, add HeroUI)
- [ ] Install dependencies
- [ ] Update vite.config.ts
- [ ] Update index.html

## Phase 2: Design System
- [ ] Create heroui-plugin.ts
- [ ] Rewrite index.css for HeroUI theming
- [ ] Delete m3e-layout.css (merge into index.css)
- [ ] Rewrite lib/theme.ts
- [ ] Delete lib/material.ts
- [ ] Update hooks/useThemeEngine.ts
- [ ] Update hooks/useVisualStyle.ts

## Phase 3: Provider & Entry Points
- [ ] Update main.tsx
- [ ] Update types.ts
- [ ] Delete custom-elements.d.ts
- [ ] Update AppContext.tsx

## Phase 4: Component Migration
- [ ] App.tsx
- [ ] ZoneSelector.tsx
- [ ] PrayerSchedule.tsx
- [ ] SettingsModal.tsx
- [ ] ClockPanel.tsx
- [ ] ThemeControl.tsx
- [ ] SharePanel.tsx
- [ ] FullCalendar.tsx
- [ ] WeatherWidget.tsx
- [ ] AzanAlert.tsx
- [ ] SolatMode.tsx
- [ ] OnboardingFlow.tsx
- [ ] MapModal.tsx
- [ ] FullWeatherModal.tsx
- [ ] LocationToast.tsx
- [ ] NotificationPrePrompt.tsx
- [ ] ErrorBoundary.tsx
- [ ] FullScreenToggle.tsx
- [ ] AnimatedDigit.tsx
- [ ] Calendar sub-components (4 files)
- [ ] Clock components (18 files)
- [ ] lib/holidays.ts
- [ ] lib/motion.ts

## Phase 5: Verification
- [ ] TypeScript compilation
- [ ] Build verification
- [ ] Unit tests
"
args.Description: "Task tracker for the HeroUI v3 migration"

args.Description: "Complete rewrite of the CSS design system for HeroUI v3. Replaced all --md-sys-color-* tokens with --app-* convenience aliases. Removed M3 typography utility classes (replaced by Tailwind text utilities), removed M3 component theming (handled by HeroUI now), merged layout rules from m3e-layout.css, and kept visual style modes (retro, glass, soft), wallpaper system, contrast modes, and motion tokens.\""

"Updated main.tsx: Removed Material Web imports and m3e-layout.css, added HeroUIProvider wrapper"

content: Now I need to tackle the massive component migration. Let me create a script that does the CSS variable replacements across all files at once. This is the most efficient approach for the 40+ files:

args.CodeContent:
"/**
 * Batch migration script: replaces all Material 3 CSS variables and component references
 * across the entire src directory to use HeroUI v3 conventions.
 * 
 * Run with: node fix-heroui-migration.cjs
 */

const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

const SRC_DIR = path.join(__dirname, 'src');

// CSS variable replacements (order matters — more specific patterns first)
const CSS_VAR_REPLACEMENTS = [
  // Surface containers
  ['var(--md-sys-color-surface-container-lowest)', 'var(--app-surface)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-surface-container-low)', 'var(--app-surface)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-surface-container-high)', 'var(--app-surface-container-high)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-surface-container-highest)', 'var(--app-surface-container-highest)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-surface-container)', 'var(--app-surface-container)'],
  
  // Surface
  ['var(--md-sys-color-surface-variant)', 'var(--app-surface-variant)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-on-surface-variant)', 'var(--app-outline)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-surface-bright)', 'var(--app-surface)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-surface-dim)', 'var(--app-surface-container)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-surface-tint)', 'var(--app-primary)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-on-surface)', 'var(--app-foreground)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-surface)', 'var(--app-surface)'],
  
  // Primary
  ['var(--md-sys-color-on-primary-container)', 'var(--app-primary)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-primary-container)', 'var(--app-primary-container, hsl(var(--heroui-primary) / 0.15))'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-on-primary)', 'var(--app-primary-foreground)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-primary)', 'var(--app-primary)'],
  
  // Secondary
  ['var(--md-sys-color-on-secondary-container)', 'var(--app-secondary)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-secondary-container)', 'var(--app-secondary-container, hsl(var(--heroui-secondary) / 0.15))'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-on-secondary)', 'var(--app-secondary-foreground)'],
  ['var(--md-sys-color-secondary)', 'var(--app-secondary)'],
  
  // Te
<truncated 5029 bytes>
args.Description: "Batch migration script to replace all M3 CSS variables, remove Material Web imports, and update typography class names across all source files"

content: Excellent! 42 files migrated. Now I need to replace the Material Web component tags (`<md-*>`) with HeroUI React components. Let me create a second script for this:

args.Description: "Component-level migration script to replace Material Web JSX tags (md-switch, md-filter-chip, md-tabs, md-icon-button, etc.) with native HTML/React equivalents styled with HeroUI tokens"

content: There are JSX syntax errors from the regex-based component migration. These are mostly malformed JSX tags from the md-* replacements. The issues are in the generated replacements for `md-filter-chip`, `md-switch`, `md-icon-button`, `md-tabs`, and `md-primary-tab`. Let me create a cleanup script to fix these:

args.Description: "Cleanup script to catch any remaining md-* tags that the regex-based migration missed"

I'm exploring non-greedy matching with `/<md-primary-tab([\s\S]*?)>/g`, but recognizing the challenges of correctly identifying tag boundaries in complex JSX. I am considering the use of simpler regex for specific files or potentially simple string replacements for some smaller files.
